name: Malama Hawai'i Makers Market
tagline: A rotating pop-up market with 150+ local artisans — the real place to shop for Hawaii-made goods, not Waikiki imports.
parking: Varies by location — check malamahawaiimarket.com for venue details
hours: 1st Sat: Hawaii Kai; 2nd & 4th Sat: Ala Moana (11am-4pm); 3rd Sat: Haleiwa
bestTime: Arrive early for the best selection; Haleiwa market has the most local-feel setting
talkStory: If you want to bring home something actually made in Hawaii — not the mass-produced 'aloha' merch from ABC Stores — Malama Hawai'i Makers Market is where locals shop. Over 150 vendors sell handmade jewelry, art, clothing, skincare, and food products designed or crafted on the islands. The market rotates between Hawaii Kai (1st Saturday), Ala Moana Center (2nd and 4th Saturdays), and Haleiwa (3rd Saturday). Talk story with the vendors — most are the actual makers. The Haleiwa location on Kamehameha Hwy has the most relaxed North Shore energy. Check their website calendar for upcoming dates.
